steps.title: Read two endpoint values from the line of best fit
steps.content:
The line of best fit spans x = 0 to x = 10 hours. Reading from the graph:
  • At $x = 0$: $y \approx 175$°F (y-intercept)
  • At $x = 10$: $y \approx 575$°F

steps.title: Compute the slope
steps.content:
$$\text{slope} = \frac{575 - 175}{10 - 0} = \frac{400}{10} = 40 \text{ °F per hour}$$
The slope is positive (line rises left to right), so temperature is increasing.

steps.title: Match the slope and direction to the choices
steps.content:
Evaluate each answer against the computed slope of +40°F/hr:
  • Choice A — 'The predicted temperature increases at a constant rate of approximately 170°F per hour': Direction is correct (increasing), but 170°F/hr is roughly the total change over 10 hours, not the per-hour rate. Off by a factor of ~10. ✗
  • Choice B — 'The predicted temperature decreases at a constant rate of approximately 170°F per hour': Direction is wrong (the line rises) and the magnitude is wrong. ✗
  • Choice C — 'The predicted temperature increases at a constant rate of approximately 40°F per hour': Direction is correct and the rate matches the computed slope of 40°F/hr. ✓
  • Choice D — 'The predicted temperature decreases at a constant rate of approximately 40°F per hour': Magnitude is correct but direction is wrong — the line slopes upward, not downward. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
